evidence.q1: Sections 1 through 15 are numbered sequentially; no internal cross-references exist that could fail to resolve.
evidence.q2: 'Personal Data', 'Processing', and 'Personal Data Breach' are each defined once in Section 2 and used consistently throughout (e.g., 'Processing' in Section 14, 'Personal Data Breach' in Sections 7 and 11).
evidence.q3: Uses precise time/quantity terms such as 'within 24 hours', 'within 2 business days', '30 days', '90 days', 'three months'; no vague phrasing like 'next quarter' or 'a reasonable while'.
evidence.q4: No archaic legal diction ('herein', 'whereas', 'thereof', etc.); language is modern and consistent with current commercial contracts.
evidence.q5: All sentences are grammatically sound; e.g., 'Vendor will notify Customer within 24 hours after discovering a Personal Data Breach.' No internal contradictions.
evidence.q6: Clause register is uniform throughout, with consistent modern phrasing: e.g., 'Vendor will process Personal Data only to provide...', 'Vendor may use subprocessors only...', 'Vendor will provide an annual SOC 2 Type II report...'.
